sourceMovie.title: Steve Jobs
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Steve Jobs for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a history rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Danny Boyle keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: King Richard
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Steve Jobs clicked for you, King Richard (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Steve Jobs?
faq.answer: Try King Richard, Richard Jewell, A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood—each shares DNA with Steve Jobs while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Steve Jobs?
faq.answer: Steve Jobs (2015) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Steve Jobs worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
